summary: Officially documented Memory Management control for DisablePagingExecutive, but Microsoft explicitly states that systems with Windows 8 and higher do not need this registry change. The key exists and is writable, but it should not be treated as an effective Windows 11 tweak.

setting.name: Disable Paging Executive
setting.casual_explanation: This setting was used for older tracing and diagnosis scenarios to keep some kernel-mode code and drivers in RAM instead of letting Windows page them out to disk. Microsoft says Windows 8 and newer systems do not need this change.
setting.why_people_change_it: People usually only touched it for older x64 tracing workflows or legacy tuning advice. It should not be treated as a normal Windows 11 performance tweak.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning it on can increase memory usage, and Microsoft explicitly says Windows 8 and later do not need this registry change for the documented tracing scenario.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft documents DisablePagingExecutive under HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Session Manager\Memory Management, but the same Kernel Trace Control API guidance explicitly says: "Systems with Windows 8 and higher do not need this registry change." The key therefore remains a real documented control surface, but not a meaningful Windows 11 optimization target.

allowed_values.label: Paging executive allowed
allowed_values.meaning: The special tracing-oriented nonpaged behavior is turned off.

allowed_values.label: Paging executive disabled
allowed_values.meaning: Drivers and kernel-mode system code are kept from being paged to disk for the documented diagnosis and tracing scenarios.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes DisablePagingExecutive = 1 on the Microsoft-documented Memory Management path.

windows_defaults.label: Normal non-diagnostics baseline
windows_defaults.applies_to: Systems that are not temporarily enabling this tracing-oriented behavior

states.rationale: Microsoft's documented command examples use 0 to turn the behavior off when the diagnosis scenario is not needed.

windows_defaults.notes: This record treats 0 as the normal off state for the documented tracing scenario and does not treat DisablePagingExecutive as a routine Windows 11 performance tweak.

recommended_profiles.label: Normal baseline
intended_for: Systems not doing the documented diagnosis workflow
avoid_for: Short-lived tracing sessions that explicitly need the behavior

states.rationale: Keep the documented diagnosis-oriented setting off when it is not needed.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want the normal baseline and do not need the extra tracing behavior.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You are following a Microsoft diagnosis workflow that explicitly requires DisablePagingExecutive = 1.
tradeoffs: The system avoids the extra memory usage of the diagnosis-oriented setting.
The special stackwalking aid described in the tracing docs is not enabled.

recommended_profiles.label: Temporary diagnosis
recommended_profiles.good_when: You are documenting or comparing an older Microsoft tracing workflow on pre-Windows-8 systems.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You are looking for a meaningful Windows 11 performance tweak or a normal user preset.
tradeoffs: The documented diagnosis-oriented behavior is enabled.
System memory usage increases and Microsoft says this should be temporary.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn: Kernel Trace Control API Reference
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents DisablePagingExecutive under HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Session Manager\Memory Management and also states that systems with Windows 8 and higher do not need this registry change.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn: ENABLE_TRACE_PARAMETERS_V1
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents that setting DisablePagingExecutive to 1 enables the stackwalking-related behavior on the documented path and warns that this should only be done for temporary diagnosis purposes because it increases system memory usage.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn: WPR Command-Line Options
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents the wpr -disablepagingexecutive option and states that turning it on prevents paging of drivers and kernel-mode system code, with Windows 7-specific tracing guidance.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes DisablePagingExecutive = 1 for the current tweak.

decision.why: Microsoft documents the exact path and values, but the same primary source also says Windows 8 and higher do not need this registry change. Keep it as a documented legacy tracing surface, not as a normal Windows 11 tuning recommendation.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: DisablePagingExecutive = 1. Systems with Windows 8 and higher do not need this registry change.
validation_proof.notes: Microsoft explicitly documents the setting and explicitly says newer Windows systems do not need it, so the record stays research-only for Windows 11 rather than moving toward Apply.
